workingDeparture.notes: Assume a Friday evening departure from Rhode Island unless later constraints force a Saturday start.

riActivation.preferredTiming: Friday morning before the road departure
riActivation.fallbackTiming: On the way out of Rhode Island Friday evening
riActivation.notes: If RI is activated Friday morning, the Friday evening reposition can focus on CT or simply making distance toward the first overnight.

parkStopPlanning.meaning: For broad planning, count two hours per non-overnight park stop to cover leaving the highway, setup, activation, teardown, and returning to the route.
parkStopPlanning.overnightParkException: If the park is also the campsite, treat the drive/setup separately and plan the activation after camp is set up.

roveCounting.multiParkRule: Multi-entity activations count once per unique POTA entity; a 2-fer counts as 2 entities.
roveCounting.eveningStrategy: Evening activations can intentionally count toward the current UTC rove day and reduce the next local day's required load.

vehicle.chargingNetworkRule: Prefer Tesla Superchargers only for planned charging stops; do not treat generic EV chargers as equivalent when judging feasibility.
vehicle.sleepingMode: Car camping in the back of the Tesla
vehicle.carCampingRule: Sleeping in the vehicle reduces setup needs but does not remove the need for a legal reserved campsite or explicit overnight permission; do not plan to sleep in ordinary day-use parking lots.
vehicle.notes: Account for roughly 30 minutes at Tesla Superchargers after each 2.5 hours of driving. Prefer parks, meals, and short breaks that can pair with Supercharger stops when practical.

campArrival.reason: Arrive with enough daylight to set up camp before full dark.
campArrival.activationTiming: For camping nights, activate the overnight park after camp is set up if needed.

weekendPlanning.notes: Saturday and Sunday have no work window, so they can carry more driving and more activations than weekdays.

constraints.jurisdictionActivationGoal: Activate every state and Washington, DC, crossed on the route.
constraints.multiParkActivationPriority: Nice to have; do not optimize the route around 2/3/4-fers unless they are convenient.

dcActivation.timing: Handle DC on the southbound pass, preferably Sunday June 21 if it fits; do not double back later just to meet the friend.
dcActivation.notes: The friend activation is nice to have, not a route constraint. If the meetup works on the pass through DC, do it; otherwise activate DC solo and keep moving south.

outreach.purpose: Contact common activators near short-listed corridor parks for practical access, parking, antenna, and operating vibe checks.
outreach.emailDraft: Hi <name>, I am planning a June 2026 POTA road trip from Rhode Island to Florida and noticed you have activated <park/reference>. I am considering it for a brief activation while passing through and would appreciate any practical advice: easy parking, good operating spots, antenna constraints, safety/access issues, and whether it is realistic for about an hour on site. Thanks and 73, Rob / N1RWJ

timelineDrafts.name: Friday evening reposition
timelineDrafts.recommendation: Working assumption: leave Rhode Island Friday around 17:00-18:00. Activating RI Friday morning is preferred if practical; otherwise activate RI on the way out.
assumptions: Approximate I-95 movement plus Tesla charging is about 27 hours before activations.
Minimum jurisdiction activation plan adds up to about 12 hours on site, but convenient multi-entity activations can reduce the number of physical stops.
Rove progress is counted by unique POTA entities on a UTC day, so evening activations can ease the next local day if they fit the UTC boundary.
Friday is treated as a work day with a 17:00-18:00 local departure from Rhode Island.
RI can be activated Friday morning before departure, which removes it from the Friday evening driving block.
Saturday and Sunday can carry heavier driving and activation loads because there is no work window.

days.focus: Evening reposition after work; activate CT near the first overnight if RI was handled Friday morning.
days.notes: Keep this short and low-risk. RI can be activated Friday morning or on the way out; a POTA camp in CT would start the camping-at-parks goal early.
Evaluate 6-10 activations, but treat 10 parks as ambitious once drive time, charging, setup, logging, meals, and fatigue are included.

days.area: New Jersey, Delaware, Maryland / DC approach
days.focus: Use the second no-work day to pick up DE/MD and do the DC friend activation Sunday evening if possible.
days.notes: End near DC/NoVA, then keep the rest of the trip moving south. Do not plan a later DC double-back.

days.focus: Begin the southbound weekday push after Sunday DC.
days.notes: Use Monday to clear VA and move toward southern VA / the NC border.

days.focus: Continue southbound rather than returning to DC.
days.notes: If Sunday DC worked, Tuesday should be a southbound workday with NC as the target.

days.focus: Keep moving through the Carolinas toward Georgia.
days.notes: The goal is to avoid leaving SC/GA and too much Florida for Friday.

days.focus: Long southern corridor day with SC and GA activations.
days.notes: Likely one of the tightest days because it combines weekday work, distance, charging, and multiple jurisdictions.

days.area: Georgia / North Florida to Singer Island
days.focus: Arrive in Florida for Field Day weekend.
days.notes: Florida activation can be near the destination if needed; avoid risking the arrival deadline.

areaBudgets.notes: Working Friday 17:00-18:00 departure block. If RI is activated Friday morning, this can be a simple CT reposition or CT activation near the first overnight; otherwise add about 1 hour for an outbound RI activation.
High-density corridor where multi-entity stops and evening UTC-day strategy could help a 10-entity rove goal.
Likely weekend work. Needs traffic-aware park choices and charging paired with food or a nearby activation.
Second weekend positioning block. If Sunday DC works, handle the friend activation here and keep the rest of the week southbound.
Best handled as a buffer/workday block around the DC evening activation rather than a high-mileage day.
A tight weekday segment because it must fit around the 13:00-17:00 work window unless the evening runs late.
Likely the hardest southern block. SC and GA should use very low-detour parks or multi-entity opportunities if available.
Useful arrival-buffer segment if Thursday gets long; can also carry the first Florida activation.
Keep the final Florida activation close to the route or destination unless earlier days create real slack.

planningConclusions: Friday evening departure is now the working assumption, with a 17:00-18:00 local departure from Rhode Island.
Activating RI Friday morning is preferred if practical because it removes RI from the outbound driving block; activating RI on the way out remains the fallback.
The northern corridor is the best place to attempt a high-count rove day because park density is high and Saturday has no work window.
The southern push from Virginia through Georgia is the schedule risk; it should prioritize low-detour, reliable access parks over high entity count.
Treat the DC friend activation as optional; DC still needs to be activated on the southbound pass, but the plan should not double back later just for the meetup.

firstWeekendPlan.utcNote: In June EDT, the UTC day changes at 20:00 local. Friday evening activations after 20:00 local count toward the next UTC day.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Hammonasset Beach State Park
fridayNightAnchors.role: standby camp anchor, not reliable for one-night advance booking
pros: Would preserve the planned Friday POTA camping night if a one-night stay becomes available
Short enough Friday drive after work
Can activate from camp after the 20:00 EDT UTC rollover
cons: CT state park campsites require a two-night minimum for advance reservations, with one-night reservations only allowed inside the 1-3 day arrival window if available
Saturday starts far enough east that Sandy Hook/Gateway makes the day long
Booking two nights for a one-night reposition is expensive and blocks a site on Saturday
15 entities is not realistic from here without several confirmed n-fers
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Best camping fit only if the booking problem is solved; otherwise treat it as an activation stop, not the planned overnight.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Silver Sands / Milford
pros: Gets meaningfully farther west Friday without a brutal evening drive
Silver Sands can be activated Friday night after UTC rollover for Saturday credit
Makes a 10-entity Saturday more comfortable if the hotel is nearby
cons: Loses the Friday POTA camping night
Still leaves several CT stops before reaching NY/NJ
Needs a hotel near the park or Supercharger corridor
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Strong current compromise for a 10-entity Saturday while keeping Sunday comfortable.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Mianus / Stamford
pros: Starts Saturday near the NY line
Can bank CT Friday night after UTC rollover
Makes Delaware reachable Saturday if the day runs well
cons: Friday evening is over two hours before any activation/setup
Skips eastern CT candidates unless they are intentionally dropped
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Best 10-entity posture among CT anchors; 12-ish entities may be possible with confirmed n-fers, but 15 remains a stretch.

fridayNightAnchors.name: Tallman / lower Hudson
pros: Starts Saturday in the NY/NJ high-density section
Can aim Saturday at NJ, DE, and maybe MD instead of spending the day crossing CT
Best setup for an aggressive 10+ entity rove
cons: Requires handling CT separately Friday or deliberately dropping CT from the rove count
fridayNightAnchors.saturdayImpact: Only anchor that makes a 15-entity thought experiment remotely plausible, and only with Friday-night UTC credit plus multiple verified n-fers.

saturdayRove.strategy: Choose the Friday anchor based on the Saturday goal. Because Hammonasset is not reliable for a one-night advance campsite reservation, Milford/Silver Sands is the best current compromise for a 10-entity UTC day, Stamford/Mianus makes Delaware reachable, and Tallman/lower Hudson is the only plausible setup for a 15-entity stretch.

timingAssumptions.source: Google Maps MCP traffic-aware route checks for Friday evening and Saturday morning; park access and operating spots still need confirmation.
timingAssumptions.fridayActivationTiming: Any Friday evening activation after 20:00 EDT counts toward the Saturday UTC rove day.
timingAssumptions.chargingStopsIncluded: 1-2 depending on anchor and southern reach

timingModels.name: Hammonasset camp, Sandy Hook included
timingModels.endAnchor: Delaware and Raritan Canal State Park
timingModels.comfort: too long for the default plan unless several stops become multi-fers
timingModels.notes: Google Maps route check at a 06:00 local Saturday departure. This only works as a camp-start model if a two-night booking is acceptable or a last-minute one-night campsite opens. Including Sandy Hook/Gateway consumes enough drive time that the baseline should probably drop Sandy Hook unless it is a verified 2-fer and the day is running early.

timingModels.name: Milford hotel, 10-entity compromise
timingModels.comfort: best current 10-entity compromise if Silver Sands is activated Friday night
timingModels.notes: Assumes Silver Sands is banked Friday evening for Saturday UTC credit, then Saturday starts with Sherwood/Mianus. The total is still a full day, but it avoids spending Saturday morning crossing eastern CT.

timingModels.name: Stamford hotel, Delaware reach
timingModels.comfort: strong 10-entity posture if CT is banked Friday night
timingModels.notes: Assumes Mianus or another CT park is activated Friday night for Saturday UTC credit, so Saturday can begin by moving directly toward NY/NJ. Fox Point is a data-backed Delaware 2-fer candidate with Northern Delaware Greenway.

timingModels.name: Tallman hotel, 15-entity thought experiment
timingModels.comfort: 15 requires Friday-night UTC credit and multiple verified n-fers
timingModels.notes: This route only makes sense if CT is handled Friday night and if Saturday's count comes from n-fers: Gateway/Coastal, Fox Point/Northern Delaware Greenway, and Fort McHenry trail overlaps. It is a stretch model, not a default Saturday.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Silver Sands plus Sherwood to Stamford hotel
fridayEveningOptions.route: Providence -> Silver Sands -> Sherwood Island -> Stamford
fridayEveningOptions.notes: Google Maps puts the driving at about 2h28 from a 17:30 Friday departure. The access problem is that CT state parks normally close at sunset, and sunset on June 19 is only about 20:28-20:31 along the Milford/Westport/Stamford corridor. Since the Saturday UTC day starts at 20:00 EDT, there is only a roughly 30-minute legal-light window unless after-hours access is confirmed.

fridayEveningOptions.name: Silver Sands plus Mianus to Stamford hotel
fridayEveningOptions.notes: This better positions Saturday at the NY line, but it pushes the hotel later and still depends on confirming legal evening access. Without such access, one full post-20:00 activation is already tight and two one-hour activations are not realistic before sunset.

saturdayRove.restOfTripComfortRule: Saturday should end at least around central/south NJ. Ending in Delaware or Maryland improves Sunday, but only if Saturday does not become a fatigue trap that compromises the rest of the week.

saturdayOvernight.reason: Use a hotel after the high-count rove day to recover and avoid starting the camping-night count before the mid-trip push.

baselineCandidates.name: Hammonasset Beach State Beach
baselineCandidates.role: activate if overnighting there or passing through early

baselineCandidates.name: Stewart B. Mckinney National Wildlife Refuge
baselineCandidates.role: early CT shoreline add-on near Hammonasset

baselineCandidates.name: Tallman Mountain State Park
baselineCandidates.role: lower-risk NY candidate north of the NYC core

baselineCandidates.name: Palisades Interstate Park
baselineCandidates.role: high-value NY/NJ corridor entity; confirm best legal operating side

baselineCandidates.name: Cheesequake State Park
baselineCandidates.role: useful NJ stop that also positions south for Sunday

stretchCandidates.name: Gateway National Recreation Area / Fort Wadsworth National Historic Site
stretchCandidates.risk: NYC/Staten Island traffic and access risk; only include after activator vibe check

stretchCandidates.name: Liberty State Park
stretchCandidates.risk: Urban access and timing risk; useful only if it does not disrupt southbound progress

stretchCandidates.name: Delaware and Raritan Canal State Park
stretchCandidates.role: southern NJ add-on or Sunday handoff candidate
stretchCandidates.risk: May fit better Sunday depending on overnight target

openQuestions: Treat Hammonasset as a standby camp option only: confirm whether a last-minute one-night campsite is possible, or decide explicitly whether booking two nights for one use is acceptable.
Confirm whether Silver Sands, Sherwood Island, or Mianus has a legal after-hours operating option such as fishing access, adjacent public right-of-way, or explicit park permission.
Confirm Gateway/Fort Wadsworth access, parking, and whether one operating position can reliably count both entities.
Find Tesla Supercharger stops that pair with late-morning CT, mid-afternoon NY/NJ, and evening NJ movement.
Contact common activators for Hammonasset, Silver Sands, Sherwood Island, Palisades, Gateway/Fort Wadsworth, and Cheesequake.

campingPolicyNotes.currentRead: Minimum-stay rules are not uniform. Car camping in the Tesla helps with setup time and site-size needs, but most public parks still require an actual campsite reservation or explicit overnight permission.
campingPolicyNotes.carCampingRead: Less-developed areas are not a workaround in Connecticut unless they are designated for overnight use. CT backpack campsites are one-night options, but they are for true backpackers, dispersed camping is not permitted, and they do not solve vehicle-adjacent sleeping. Highway service plazas/rest areas may be viable rest/sleep stops in the vehicle, but they are not POTA park camping and should be treated as safety rest rather than a campsite.

jurisdictions.summary: CT state park campsites generally require two-night advance reservations, with one-night campsite reservations only inside the 1-3 day arrival window if available. Day-use parks generally close at sunset.
At least the relevant Harriman/Beaver Pond camping brochure says campsite reservations are minimum two nights; day-of arrivals may register subject to availability.
Maryland requires two-night weekend-linked stays from Memorial Day weekend through Labor Day, with three-night holiday-weekend rules.

North Carolina has a two-night minimum between Friday and Sunday on holiday weekends from Memorial Day through Labor Day, but the rule is waived for bookings within 7 days of arrival; some walk-up/onsite options exist.

jurisdictions.risk: lower for campsites, higher for late arrival logistics
jurisdictions.summary: Florida reservation information lists maximum stay rules and cabin two-night minimums, but not a general campsite minimum in the checked source. Late arrivals after sunset require calling the park before 5 p.m. for gate access.
Need park-specific reservation checks before relying on one-night stays. Do not assume the CT rule applies everywhere, but also do not assume one-night stays are available on summer weekends.

nferEvidence.sourceDescription: Candidate n-fer locations inferred from submissions by top activators.
nferEvidence.currentRead: Useful for finding proven multi-reference positions, but treat it as evidence to verify rather than proof that an activation will work from any exact parking spot.

saturday.summary: The data does not validate the assumed Gateway/Fort Wadsworth 2-fer.
saturday.notes: The current Saturday stretch should not rely on this pair until activator outreach or another source confirms it.

saturday.summary: Gateway National Recreation Area plus New Jersey Coastal State Trail appears as a data-backed 2-fer.
saturday.routingNote: Google Maps routing from Palisades to Sandy Hook Gateway to Cheesequake is about 2h total drive time, so this may be too expensive for the Saturday UTC target unless it replaces the Staten Island idea and the day is running early.

sunday.summary: Fox Point State Park plus Northern Delaware Greenway National Recreation Trail is a Delaware 2-fer candidate near I-95.
Fort McHenry has repeated n-fer evidence with Star-Spangled Banner and Captain John Smith Chesapeake trails.
sunday.routingNote: Central NJ to Fox Point to Fort McHenry to Rock Creek to Mason Neck is about 4h31m of driving before activation time.

sunday.summary: Mason Neck State Park plus Elizabeth Hartwell Mason Neck NWR is a Virginia 2-fer candidate for the Sunday camp concept.
sunday.routingNote: Rock Creek Park to Mason Neck State Park is roughly 55-63 minutes depending on the exact route.

assumptions: Saturday night is a hotel in central or south New Jersey.
Broad planning now uses 2 hours per non-overnight park stop, including detour, setup, activation, teardown, and return to the highway.
Camping nights should arrive by about 18:30-19:00 local for setup before dark.
A campsite activation can happen after setup, effectively as a night activation.
The plan should avoid leaving a very long Georgia-to-Singer-Island push for Friday.
With the 2-hour park-stop rule, broad overnight choices should be driven by where a campsite can replace a separate daytime jurisdiction stop without breaking the southbound flow.

overnightStrategy.goal: Use camping nights where they can cover a required jurisdiction without consuming a separate 2-hour daytime park stop, and use hotels on structurally long or schedule-sensitive days.
overnightStrategy.summary: Because CT shoreline state park campsites are not reliable for a one-night Friday reservation, the revised pattern moves Friday to a hotel and keeps four camping nights by using two two-night-or-less camp blocks later in the week: Sunday/Monday and Wednesday/Thursday.

nights.why: Hammonasset and similar CT shoreline campsites are not reliable for one-night advance booking. Use Friday as a hotel reposition night and activate a nearby CT/NY park after the UTC rollover.
After the high-count Saturday rove, a hotel avoids a camp-setup deadline and keeps Sunday's DE/MD/DC day comfortable.
If the Sunday camp is close enough to the DC activation, it can bank Virginia without spending Monday on a separate Virginia stop.

Adding Monday as a camp night replaces the lost Friday camp while staying within the two-consecutive-camp limit after Sunday. The campsite should cover North Carolina so Tuesday does not need a separate NC stop.
Tuesday should reset after the Sunday/Monday camp block. Use a daytime South Carolina activation, then hotel farther south to protect the Wednesday/Thursday camp block.
Wednesday becomes the first night of the second camp block and can cover Georgia or a north Florida approach park, depending on exact routing.

nights.area: Central Florida or Treasure Coast approach
nights.why: A Florida POTA campsite lets Florida get activated after setup on Thursday and turns Friday into a shorter, lower-stress destination day.

overnightStrategy.fallbackNote: If the Sunday Virginia camp, Monday North Carolina camp, Wednesday Georgia/north Florida camp, or Thursday Florida camp proves impractical, replace it with a hotel and add back the corresponding 2-hour daytime jurisdiction stop elsewhere in the route.

days.focus: Pick up Delaware and Maryland, activate DC on the southbound pass, and finish at a Virginia campsite.
days.notes: Sunday is long but feasible because there is no work window. The friend meetup can happen here if it fits, but the hard requirement is activating DC and still reaching the Virginia campsite in time for setup.

days.focus: Use the workday margins for southbound transit and make the North Carolina activation from camp after setup.
days.notes: This replaces the lost Friday camp night while staying within the two-consecutive-camp limit. The campsite should be far enough south that Tuesday can still make South Carolina / Georgia progress.

days.focus: Use a daytime stop for South Carolina, then hotel farther south to reset after two camp nights.
days.notes: North Carolina should already be covered by the Monday campsite, so Tuesday's separate park stop can focus on South Carolina.

days.focus: Use Wednesday to make Georgia progress and cover the overnight jurisdiction from camp.
days.notes: This is the first night of the second camp block. If the campsite is in north Florida instead of Georgia, add a low-detour Georgia stop earlier in the day.

days.focus: Use Thursday to move through Florida and make the overnight Florida activation from camp.
days.notes: This preserves the low-stress Friday arrival while still banking Florida before destination day.

days.focus: Destination day and hotel arrival for Field Day weekend, with Florida already covered the night before.
days.notes: If Thursday successfully banks Florida from camp, Friday becomes a short arrival day instead of another stop-and-drive day.

restOfTripDraft.risk: The broad plan works only if DC is handled on the southbound pass, the route stays monotonic afterward, and the Sunday Virginia campsite is close enough that camp setup is still realistic after the DC activation.

overnight.name: TBD — somewhere in CT or NY

days.notes: Candidate Day 1 if departing Saturday. Friday departure remains possible; fill in park targets once clusters are planned.
